questions.question: Explain the evolution of GPUs from fixed-function graphics hardware to general-purpose computing devices.
keyPoints: Fixed-function pipeline era for vertex/fragment processing
Programmable shader stage enabling limited compute via graphics APIs
CUDA (2006) providing direct C-based GPU programming without graphics metaphors

questions.question: Compare CPU and GPU architectures in detail. Discuss their design philosophies, strengths, and the types of workloads each is best suited for.
keyPoints: CPU optimizes for latency with large caches, branch prediction, out-of-order execution, few powerful cores
GPU optimizes for throughput with thousands of simple cores, high memory bandwidth, hardware multithreading
GPUs excel at data-parallel, compute-intensive, regular memory access workloads
CPUs excel at complex branching, serial algorithms, irregular memory access, small datasets
SIMT model in GPUs vs complex control flow in CPUs
